conversations.content: Let's do some physics exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.
Please calculate using the previously mentioned special rules of the Azeroth world: A wave has a frequency f=50Hz and a wavelength λ=2m, what is its wave speed v?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The previously mentioned special rules of the Azeroth world, given frequency f and wavelength λ, find wave speed v</content>
Related memory fragments: None. Low related memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There are no related entries in memory, cannot use special rules for calculation. Here we use the conventional formula, v = f × λ, substituting the values: v = 50 × 2 = 100 m/s.
conversations.content: Sorry, I did not recall the calculation method for wave speed under the Azeroth special rules. Under the conventional method, v = f × λ = 50 × 2 = 100 m/s.
It's okay, this problem indeed did not mention any special rules.
Okay, I will continue to work hard to memorize each special rule.
